title: Shopping at 7-Eleven
description: Buy snacks and a drink, use your Octopus card to pay
context: Hong Kong has over 1,000 convenience stores — 7-Eleven and Circle K on almost every corner. You can pay bills, top up Octopus cards, and pick up a hot meal. Staff are used to being busy; keep it short and friendly.
culturalFact: Hong Kong has the highest density of 7-Eleven stores in the world — roughly one per 6,000 residents. The stores never close and many locals use them as a second kitchen, grabbing instant noodles or a hot dog for breakfast.

lines.speaker: you
lines.jyutping: m4 goi1, ni1 go3 gei2 do1 cin2?
lines.english: Excuse me, how much is this?

lines.speaker: you
lines.jyutping: hou2, zung6 jiu3 jat1 zi1 seoi2.
lines.english: OK, and a bottle of water too.

lines.speaker: staff
lines.jyutping: jat1 gung6 sap6 sei3 man1 bun3.

lines.speaker: you
lines.jyutping: jung6 baat3 daat6 tung1 dak1 m4 dak1?
lines.english: Can I pay with Octopus card?

lines.speaker: staff
lines.jyutping: dak1 ga3, bit1 jat1 bit1 laa3.
lines.english: Yes, go ahead and tap it.
